$note: Captured snapshot of the COMPARED forecast/intelligence product for the azimuth benchmark (synthesis/benchmark.py). Quoted by product + capture date; NOT surfaced as an azimuth channel and NOT an L1 source. Refresh with pull_benchmark_foils.py.

foil.title: Inflation and rates pressure from Venezuela infrastructure fragility state
foil.scenario_short: Venezuela infrastructure fragility state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through energy supply shock, with 52% pressure and 64% state confirmation. For now, the base case stays near 59% over the 30d. Actors...
foil.perspective_strategic: Venezuela infrastructure fragility state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through energy supply shock, with 52% pressure and 64% state confirmation is setting the strategic baseline, and the current 56% probability implies a live but not settled risk path.
foil.perspective_contrarian: The current 75% confidence level still leaves room for reversal. If that restraint persists, the forecast can move below the current 56% baseline. Actors prioritize containment and the system drifts toward stabilization unless new hard signals emerge.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Venezuela becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 56%.
foil.top_signal: Venezuela infrastructure fragility state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through energy supply shock, with 52% pressure and 64% state confirmation

foil.title: Inflation and rates pressure from Persian Gulf maritime disruption state
foil.scenario_short: Inflation and rates pressure from Persian Gulf maritime disruption has a 60% probability, driven by shipping cost shock with 52% pressure and 73% state confirmation, but the trend is falling.
foil.perspective_strategic: The 60% probability of inflation and rates pressure, stemming from 52% shipping cost shock and 73% state confirmation, is significant, but the falling trend indicates a potential easing of risk.
foil.perspective_contrarian: The observed trend is already easing from 65% to 60%, and if this momentum continues, the outcome could be a stabilization below the current 60% baseline, especially without new direct reporting.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Persian Gulf becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 60%.
foil.top_signal: Persian Gulf maritime disruption state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through shipping cost shock, with 52% pressure and 73% state confirmation

seismic-infrastructure.absence_reason: No product publishes a seismic forecast — earthquakes are not predictable, so there is no forecast/intel alternative to azimuth's observed USGS record. This is the honest inverse: for an observed natural-hazard fact, the sourced record IS the only trustworthy source.
